messages.content:
You will be given the current section title and the current page_text.
    Your job is to check if the current section starts in the beginning of the given page_text.
    If there are other contents before the current section title,
    then the current section does not start in the beginning of the given page_text.
    If the current section title is the first content in the given page_text,
    then the current section starts in the beginning of the given page_text.

    Note: do fuzzy matching, ignore any space inconsistency in the page_text.

    The given section title is 媒体声音.
    The given page_text is 79媒体声音
罗斯基、龙虾游戏推荐、急速行走的小 U盘、一枚游戏干饭人
.

    reply format:
    {
        "start_begin": "yes or no" (yes if the section starts in the beginning of the page_text, no otherwise)
    }
    Directly return the final JSON structure. Do not output anything else.

Do not include any thinking field, explanation, or reasoning. Return only the final requested JSON or text.

message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to check if the section title "媒体声音" starts at the beginning of the page_text "79媒体声音\n罗斯基、龙虾游戏推荐、急速行走的小 U盘、一枚游戏干饭人\n.".
The page_text starts with "79媒体声音".
The section title is "媒体声音".
There is a number "79" before "媒体声音".
So the section title is not the first content. It has "79" before it.
Therefore, it does not start at the beginning.
Wait, "79" might be a page number. The prompt says "If there are other contents before the current section title, then the current section does not start in the beginning of the given page_text."
So "79" is other content.
Thus, start_begin should be "no".
Let's double check. "79媒体声音" -> "79" is before "媒体声音".
So answer is no.
Format: {"start_begin": "no"}
Done. 
Check fuzzy matching: ignore space inconsistency. But "79" is clearly not a space.
So "no".
Return JSON. 
Proceed. 
Output: {"start_begin": "no"}
